case.title: MAKAH INDIAN TRIBE v. TAX COMMISSION OF WASHINGTON

opinion.h4: MAKAH INDIAN TRIBE v. TAX COMMISSION OF WASHINGTON, (1968)
MAKAH INDIAN TRIBE v. TAX COMMISSION OF WASHINGTON ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 52. Decided October 14, 1968.
opinion.p: John J. O'Connell, Attorney General of Washington, and Timothy R. Malone and J. Richard Duggan, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee Tax Commission of Washington.
Solicitor General Griswold, Assistant Attorney General Martz, and Roger P. Marquis for the United States, as amicus curiae.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: DESERT OUTDOOR ADVERT., INC. v. COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO, (1968)
opinion.p: DESERT OUTDOOR ADVERTISING, INC. v. COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, FOURTH APPELLATE DISTRICT. No. 114. Decided October 14, 1968.
255 Cal. App. 2d 765, 63 Cal. Rptr. 543, appeal dismissed.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
